context_note: Canada vs Qatar, WC2026 Group B, Round 2 (BC Place, Vancouver). Canada drew 1-1 vs BIH in R1, Qatar drew 1-1 vs SUI in R1. Canada heavy favorite at home (FTR 76.63%). Qatar 7.55% win prob. Canada needs a win to advance comfortably. Referee: Juan Benitez (PAR) - CONMEBOL ref, assumed average card rate (~3.5 YC/game) without specific stats. Both lineups confirmed starting: David (CAN), Afif (QAT).

referee.name: Juan Benitez
referee.card_rate: assumed avg ~3.5 YC/game (no specific data available - worldfootball.net Cloudflare blocked)
referee.note: CONMEBOL refs at WC tend to be average in terms of card issuance. No upward or downward adjustment applied to card estimates.

espn_qatar_historical.note: Qatar vs strong opposition (n=5: El Salvador, Ireland, Ecuador, Senegal, Netherlands): avg SOT=1.6, 2H SOT avg=1.2. In WC2026 R1 vs SUI: 3 SOT (better than avg vs strong). 373 scoreless minutes context but they DID score vs SUI.

canada_fouls_historical.note: Canada plays at home (BC Place). Home context suggests ~10-11 fouls/match. WC2026 R1 confirms 10 fouls vs Bosnia.

Q1.question: Will Qatar commit more fouls than Canada?
Q1.method: Skellam: λ_QAT=11.8 (blended tournament avg: WC2026 R1=12, WC2022=10.33, AFC2024=12.7), λ_CAN=10.5 (tournament context: WC2026 R1=10 fouls, footystats home avg=10.75). Skellam mean difference=1.3 → P(QAT>CAN) ≈ 0.59. Market cross-check: Smarkets Most Cards = Qatar 53.92% vs Canada 21.75% (1:2.5 ratio strongly signals Qatar more aggressive/physical). Qatar as heavy underdog (7.55% win prob) will sit deep and foul more to disrupt Canada's high-possession game. Similar to CAN-BIH where BIH (20 fouls) > CAN (10 fouls). Slight upward shade for heavy underdog foul inflation.
Q1.rule_check: RULE8 fires minimally (draw 16.14%, low) - no shrinkage needed toward 50%

Q2.question: Will there be 4 or more total shots on target in the second half?
Q2.method: Poisson compound: λ_CAN_SOT_full≈6.5 (from CAN SOT OU4.5 = 74.66% over → Poisson solve), λ_CAN_SOT_2H = 6.5×0.50 = 3.25. λ_QAT_SOT_full≈2.5 (from QAT SOT OU2.5 = 46.47% over → Poisson: λ≈2.55), λ_QAT_SOT_2H = 2.55×0.50 = 1.275. Combined 2H λ = 3.25+1.275 = 4.525. P(X≥4, λ=4.525) = 0.658. Slight shade down for Qatar 2H SOT uncertainty. Cross-check: CAN-BIH R1 had 4+3=7 SOT, question won at 0.70 estimate. CAN-QAT should have more SOT on both sides.

Q3.question: Will a penalty kick be awarded OR a red card be shown?
Q3.method: P(penalty)=0.2914 (direct Smarkets market). P(RC): from Cards OU1.5=83.01%, OU2.5=62.60% → λ_cards≈3.2 total cards expected. Historical WC RC rate ~12-15% per match. P(RC)≈0.13. Compound independence: P(pen OR RC) = 0.2914+0.13-0.2914*0.13 = 0.384. Qatar is physical underdog (Most Cards Qatar 53.92%), slight RC elevation. Average referee (Benitez PAR, no specific data).

Q4.question: Will Jonathan David have at least 1 shot on target in the second half?
Q4.method: Full-match David SOT market = 0.6840 (liquid). λ_david_full = -ln(1-0.6840) = 1.152. λ_david_2H = 1.152 × 0.50 = 0.576. P(David 2H SOT ≥ 1) = 1-e^(-0.576) = 0.438. RULE15 check: David is on WINNING team (Canada 76.63%) → RULE15 does NOT apply (RULE15 = losing team's forward suppressed). Blowout winner adjustment: for FORWARDS (not midfielders), counter-attacking opportunities may increase if Qatar chases → neutral to slight positive adjustment. David confirmed starting. Slight up-shade from 0.438 for counter-attack bonus in potential blowout.
Q4.blowout_winner_note: David is FORWARD (not midfielder) - counter-attack bonus applies vs midfielder Xhaka adjustment

Q5.question: Will Canada win the match?
Q5.method: Direct Smarkets FTR Canada = 0.7663. Tier 1 market, no deviation. Canada is heavy home favorite, needs win to advance. Market reflects all public information.

Q6.question: At halftime, will Canada be winning?
Q6.method: Direct Smarkets HT result: Canada = 0.5848. Direct market match. Cross-check: 1H AH Canada -0.5 = 0.5630 (consistent). Canada leads HT 58-59% chance.

Q7.question: Will Canada have 5 or more shots on target?
Q7.method: Direct Smarkets CAN SOT Over 4.5 = 0.7466. This is an EXACT match to the question (5+ SOT = over 4.5). Tier 1 market, no deviation.

Q8.question: Will Qatar have 5 or more corner kicks?
Q8.method: Direct Smarkets QAT corners Over 4.5 = 0.1338. Exact match. Qatar was expected to get only ~2-3 corners (got 3 in R1 vs SUI). Heavy underdog, minimal territorial pressure expected. This is in the 'underdog 5+ corners' family (similar to CAN-BIH BIH 5+ corners = 0.27 market/estimate, outcome NO → +9.46). Market already very bearish at 13.38%.
Q8.analogy: CAN-BIH BIH 5+ corners = 0.265 mid, we submitted 0.27, outcome NO

Q9.question: Will Akram Afif have at least 1 shot on target?
Q9.method: Market: Afif SOT = 0.2899 (liquid). RULE15 applies: Afif is the expected LOSER team's (Qatar 7.55%) primary forward. Historical RULE15 validation: Fajardo (lost 0-1, 0 SOT), Sučić (lost 4-2, 0 SOT), Džeko (lost 4-1, 0 SOT) = 3/3 confirmed. In QAT-SUI (our submission), we went near-market at 0.42 and Afif had 0 SOT (+7.26). This time market is already at 0.29 (already moved from 0.43 in QAT-SUI), so RULE15 is partially priced in. Half-RULE15 shade: 0.2899 - 0.07 ≈ 0.22. But CAN is an even bigger favorite than SUI was vs QAT, so full RULE15 may be warranted.
Q9.rule15_evidence: 3/3 confirmed (Fajardo GHA-PAN, Sucic ENG-CRO, Dzeko SUI-BIH). Market has already moved from 0.43 to 0.29.

Q10.question: Will Qatar score in the second half?
Q10.method: Direct Smarkets 2H QAT OU0.5 goals = 0.3117. Exact match. Qatar scored in R1 vs SUI (1-1 final). λ_QAT_2H = -ln(1-0.3117) = 0.373. Market already reflects Qatar as extreme underdog. Qatar desperation/chasing game may generate some late chances (historical pattern: Qatar's 2H SOT in WC2022 vs Senegal = 3 when trailing).

key_rules_applied.RULE15: Q9 (Afif) - expected loser's primary forward shaded DOWN from market 0.2899 → 0.22. 3/3 validated rule.
key_rules_applied.RULE_tier1_no_deviation: Q5, Q6, Q7, Q8, Q10 - all direct Smarkets market reads, no deviation applied.
key_rules_applied.RULE_blowout_winner_forward: Q4 (David) - on WINNING team, FORWARD (not midfielder), no penalty. Counter-attack bonus slight upside.
key_rules_applied.RULE_skellam_fouls: Q1 - λ_QAT=11.8 vs λ_CAN=10.5, Skellam gives 0.59, rounded to 0.60 with Most Cards market support.
key_rules_applied.RULE_no_2plus_offsides_family: No offsides question in this match (no offside props visible in the 10 questions).

Q2_4plus_2h_sot.notes: CAN 10 total SOT (all CAN, QAT 0). CAN scored 3 in 2H. Inference: CAN had 4+ SOT in 2H. HIGH CONFIDENCE YES. Confirm from QK.

Q4_david_2h_sot.notes: UNKNOWN - no player data from ESPN. CAN had 10 SOT total. Confirm from QK.

Q9_afif_sot_rule15.notes: QAT 0 total SOT. Afif got 0. outcome=NO

Q2_4plus_2H_total_SOT.notes: CONFIRMED NO. Canada scored 3 in 2H but only had 3 SOT in 2H (3 goals from 3 SOT = 100% conversion). Qatar 0 SOT. Total 2H SOT=3 < 4.

Q4_David_1plus_2H_SOT.notes: CONFIRMED YES. David had 1+ SOT in 2H. Crowd 50% closer than our 44%.

Q8_Qatar_5plus_corners.notes: Qatar 1 corner only. Market 0.1338 was spot on.

Q9_Afif_1plus_SOT.notes: Afif 0 SOT (Qatar 0 total SOT). RULE15 confirmed n=4.

post_match_results.learning_notes: Q7 Canada 5+ SOT (+15.27) confirmed direct-market trust pays off. Q9 Afif RULE15 (+14.81) = 4th confirmed RULE15 case. Q4 David (-3.50) = counter-attack forward on winning team still generates SOT even in blowout. Q2 4+ 2H SOT (NO) = 3 goals from 3 SOT = 100% conversion rate; extreme efficiency means fewer total SOT than expected from goals.
